summary: Backend: 33/33 pytest tests pass (20 existing + 13 new iteration-2). Found & fixed 1 critical bug: password reset 500 error due to naive vs aware datetime comparison in /api/auth/reset-password. Frontend: all new admin pages (Trained Answers, Knowledge Gaps, AI Config fallback, Tickets delete) load with correct data-testids. End-user flow verified: registration → ask unknown question → AI responds naturally (no forced 'Explanation:/Steps:' headers) → fallback 'Get Help' (configured name) button appears → unanswered question tracked in admin Gap Analysis. Only remaining issue: 'Made with Emergent' badge is still visible in UI despite requirement to hide it.

ui_bugs.issue: 'Made with Emergent' badge is visible on user portal, admin dashboard, and login page. Requirement says it should be hidden via CSS. Computed style returns display:block, visibility:visible, opacity:1 — the CSS rule to hide it is missing or not applied.

action_items: Hide the 'Made with Emergent' badge. Either add CSS rule like `a[href*='emergent.sh'] { display: none !important; }` in index.css/App.css or remove the script that injects it from public/index.html. Currently visible across the app despite requirement.
(Already fixed by me) /app/backend/routes/auth_routes.py reset_password — normalized datetime comparison to handle MongoDB's naive datetimes. Without this fix, every valid reset attempt returned 500.
critical_code_review_comments: CRITICAL BUG FIXED: reset_password compared `datetime.now(timezone.utc)` (aware) to `token_doc['expires_at']` (naive from MongoDB) → TypeError 500. Same pattern exists in `auth.py` (login_attempts.locked_until) but Mongo can sometimes return aware datetimes if pymongo `tz_aware=True`; ensure the Mongo client is configured consistently, or normalize on read everywhere.
/api/auth/forgot-password returns the reset_token in the response body. Acceptable in dev (no email service), but for production must remove this and only log it / send via email.
/api/admin/unanswered/bulk-delete uses raw request.json() and ObjectId(i) without try/except — sending a malformed id will throw bson.errors.InvalidId and 500. Validate or wrap.

Trained answer text search uses `$text` index but no filter for case where text index creation failed silently (try/except in server.py). The fallback regex approach in chat_routes only runs if $text throws. Consider explicit index existence check.
Unanswered questions are tracked but the 'mark-added' endpoint only flips status; there is no link/foreign key to the created knowledge item, making auditability harder.

fallback-config endpoint requires auth — fine, but it forces an extra round trip per session; consider embedding the fallback values directly in the SSE `done` event (which already happens) to avoid the extra GET on portal load.
auth_routes.user_out reads `user['_id']` assuming key always exists; works because all callers always supply it, but defensive .get would be safer.
CORS_ORIGINS='*' with allow_credentials=True remains (carryover from iteration 1).

success_rate.frontend: ~95% (all 5 new feature flows pass; only Made-with-Emergent badge visibility bug remains)

test_credentials: Admin: admin@biziverse.com / Admin@123 — verified still working at end of suite (zzz test restores via forgot-password if needed).
seed_data_creation: Created TEST_-prefixed unanswered questions (some cleaned via bulk-delete), trained answers (cleaned in teardown), tickets (cleaned in delete test), users (left in DB). AI Config fallback fields were updated by tests: fallback_message='TEST_fallback msg ...' and fallback_button_text='Get Help' / 'Open Ticket'. Main agent may want to restore default values on the AI Config screen.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend regression suite at /app/backend/tests/ now has two files: backend_test.py (iter1) and iteration2_test.py (iter2). All 33 tests pass. Password reset bug was fixed by adding tz normalization. The 'Made with Emergent' badge is the only outstanding bug; verify CSS hiding once main agent applies fix. AI Config fallback_message/button_text were modified by tests — current values: fallback_message starts with 'TEST_fallback msg', fallback_button_text='Open Ticket' (or 'Get Help' after UI test). Reset to defaults if needed.
rca of the issue: Password reset 500 RCA: motor (pymongo async) by default returns naive datetimes (no tzinfo) for stored UTC dates unless the client is constructed with tz_aware=True. The handler stored `expires_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc) + timedelta(hours=1)` (aware), but on read MongoDB returned it naive, then `datetime.now(timezone.utc) > token_doc['expires_at']` raises TypeError. Fix: on read, do `if expires_at.tzinfo is None: expires_at = expires_at.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc)` before compare. Reproduction: POST /api/auth/forgot-password → grab token → POST /api/auth/reset-password with that token → 500 prior to fix, 200 after.
